content.text:
| MCP:A92mnH | # Fastapi Project Template
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | A FastAPI project template using Make, Docker Compose and Github Actions,
| MCP:A92mnH | that includes **SQLAlchemy**, **Alembic** and an integration test setup.
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | [![Code Integration](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml)
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ##  Installation
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | The only requirements to develop are **Docker** and **Make**.
| MCP:A92mnH | Docker makes it easy to get started and enables easier switching between projects, operating systems and machines.
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | To start up the project locally you first clone the project, and then run the following command in the cloned directory:
| MCP:A92mnH | ```sh
| MCP:A92mnH | $ git clone https://github.com/Donnype/fastapi-template.git
| MCP:A92mnH | $ cd fastapi-template
| MCP:A92mnH | $ make up
| MCP:A92mnH | ```
| MCP:A92mnH | Then to update the schema using alembic, in another shell:
| MCP:A92mnH | ```sh
| MCP:A92mnH | $ make migrate
| MCP:A92mnH | ```
| MCP:A92mnH | **That's it**. The app is set up and should be running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:A92mnH | Code changes are automatically detected using a mounted docker volume.
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ### Makefile
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ---
| MCP:A92mnH | The Makefile is the 'entrypoint' for the tools in this structure,
| MCP:A92mnH | such that you can easily run different commands without remembering the exact arguments.
| MCP:A92mnH | Run `make help` to get an overview of the available commands:
| MCP:A92mnH | ```sh
| MCP:A92mnH | $ make help
| MCP:A92mnH | up:
| MCP:A92mnH |  Run the application
| MCP:A92mnH | done: check test
| MCP:A92mnH |  Prepare for a commit
| MCP:A92mnH | test: utest itest
| MCP:A92mnH |  Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:A92mnH | utest: cleantest
| MCP:A92mnH |  Run unit tests
| MCP:A92mnH | itest: cleantest
| MCP:A92mnH |  Run integration tests
| MCP:A92mnH | check: cleantest
| MCP:A92mnH |  Check the code base
| MCP:A92mnH | cleantest:
| MCP:A92mnH |  Clean up test containers
| MCP:A92mnH | migrations:
| MCP:A92mnH |  Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:A92mnH | migrate:
| MCP:A92mnH |  Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:A92mnH | downgrade:
| MCP:A92mnH |  Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:A92mnH | help:
| MCP:A92mnH |  Display this help message
| MCP:A92mnH | ```
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | Make allows you to collect common scripts and commands for the project.
| MCP:A92mnH | Other projects using different programming languages and frameworks get the same development interface by using Make.
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ---
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ### Installation without Docker
| MCP:A92mnH | To install a local copy of the python environment (to get code-completion for example),
| MCP:A92mnH | you must have **Poetry** installed.
| MCP:A92mnH | Create a poetry environment by running `poetry install` at the root of the project.
| MCP:A92mnH | To start up the server in the poetry environment and talk to a test sqlite database in `./test.db`, run
| MCP:A92mnH | ```bash
| MCP:A92mnH | $ export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
| MCP:A92mnH | $ poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
| MCP:A92mnH | ```
| MCP:A92mnH | Again, the app should be all set up and running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ### Tests
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ---
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | The tests are containerized and the Docker setup can be found in the `.ci/` folder.
| MCP:A92mnH | They are written using Pytest.
| MCP:A92mnH | You can run the tests using:
| MCP:A92mnH | ```bash
| MCP:A92mnH | $ make test
| MCP:A92mnH | ```
| MCP:A92mnH | This runs the integration & unit tests.
| MCP:A92mnH | If you want to run them separately, use `make itest` to run the integration tests and `make utest` to run the unit tests.
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ## Further reading
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | To read about the benefits of using this template,
| MCP:A92mnH | check out [my blog post](https://bitestreams.com/blog/fastapi_template/).
| MCP:A92mnH | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: d202c38a9dd8172e76072e244ad5292d61b67435)

| MCP:A92mnH | SHELL := bash
| MCP:A92mnH | .ONESHELL:
| MCP:A92mnH | .SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1
| MCP:A92mnH | export COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=1
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | targets: help
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | up: ## Run the application
| MCP:A92mnH | 	docker compose up -d --build api
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | done: check test ## Prepare for a commit
| MCP:A92mnH | test: utest itest  ## Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ci-compose := docker compose -f .ci/docker-compose.yml
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | utest: cleantest ## Run unit tests
| MCP:A92mnH | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pytest -m unit .
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | itest: cleantest ## Run integration tests
| MCP:A92mnH | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm integration pytest -m integration .
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | check: cleantest ## Check the code base
| MCP:A92mnH | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pre-commit run -a
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | cleantest:  ## Clean up test containers
| MCP:A92mnH | 	$(ci-compose) kill
| MCP:A92mnH | 	$(ci-compose) down --remove-orphans
| MCP:A92mnH | 	$(ci-compose) build
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | ## Migrations
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | migrations: ## Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:A92mnH | ifeq ($(m),)
| MCP:A92mnH | 	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
| MCP:A92mnH | else ifeq ($(revid),)
| MCP:A92mnH | 	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
| MCP:A92mnH | else
| MCP:A92mnH | 	docker compose run api alembic revision --autogenerate -m "$(m)" --rev-id="$(revid)"
| MCP:A92mnH | endif
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | migrate: ## Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:A92mnH | 	docker compose run --rm api alembic upgrade head
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | downgrade: ## Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:A92mnH | 	docker compose run --rm api alembic downgrade -1
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | help: ## Display this help message
| MCP:A92mnH | 	@awk -F '##' '/^[a-z_]+:[a-z ]+##/ { print "\033[34m"$$1"\033[0m" "\n" $$2 }' Makefile

| MCP:A92mnH | version: "3.7"
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | services:
| MCP:A92mnH |   api:
| MCP:A92mnH |     depends_on:
| MCP:A92mnH |       - db
| MCP:A92mnH |     ports:
| MCP:A92mnH |       - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000"
| MCP:A92mnH |     build:
| MCP:A92mnH |       dockerfile: ./api/Dockerfile
| MCP:A92mnH |       context: .
| MCP:A92mnH |     volumes:
| MCP:A92mnH |       - ./api:/home/user/app/api
| MCP:A92mnH |     environment:
| MCP:A92mnH |       - DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@db:5432/postgres
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH |   db:
| MCP:A92mnH |     image: postgres:14.5
| MCP:A92mnH |     ports:
| MCP:A92mnH |       - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
| MCP:A92mnH |     volumes:
| MCP:A92mnH |       - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
| MCP:A92mnH |     environment:
| MCP:A92mnH |       - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test
| MCP:A92mnH | 
| MCP:A92mnH | volumes:
| MCP:A92mnH |   postgres-data:

data.content:
Based on the repository contents, here's how to start this FastAPI project locally with Docker and apply database migrations:

## 🚀 Starting the Project Locally with Docker

1. **Clone the repository**:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T04_R01_C01_A03.git
   cd T04_R01_C01_A03
   ```

2. **Start the application**:
   ```bash
   make up
   ```
   This command runs `docker compose up -d --build api`, which:
   - Builds the API container using `./api/Dockerfile`
   - Starts a PostgreSQL database container (`db`)
   - Mounts the `./api` directory for live code reloading
   - Exposes the API on `http://localhost:5000`

3. **Access the application**:
   Once running, visit **[http://localhost:5000/docs](http://localhost:5000/docs)** to see the API documentation.

## 🗄️ Applying Database Migrations

The project uses **Alembic** for database migrations with SQLAlchemy.

1. **Run pending migrations**:
   ```bash
   make migrate
   ```
   This executes `docker compose run --rm api alembic upgrade head`, which applies all unapplied migrations to the database.

2. **Generate new migrations** (when you change models):
   ```bash
   make migrations m="description of changes" revid="0001"
   ```
   - `m`: Migration description (required)
   - `revid`: Revision ID (e.g., "0001", required)

3. **Downgrade migrations** (if needed):
   ```bash
   make downgrade
   ```

## 📋 Key Details

- **Database**: PostgreSQL (credentials: `postgres:test@db:5432/postgres`)
- **Environment**: Docker Compose manages both API and database services
- **Live Reloading**: Code changes in `./api` are automatically detected
- **Local Development**: For non-Docker setup, use Poetry with `export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/` and run `poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload`

The Makefile provides all necessary commands - run `make help` for a full list of available targets.
